Title: Verify patient dashboard renders correctly on Chrome latest on Windows 11
Preconditions: Test environment available with OpenMRS RefApp; valid clinician account; Chrome latest installed on Windows 11
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Chrome latest on Windows 11 | 2. Navigate to https://o2.openmrs.org | 3. Log in as clinician | 4. Open a patient with an active visit | 5. Inspect dashboard widgets, fonts, layout
Expected_Result: Patient dashboard renders without layout breakage; all widgets visible; no console errors; fonts and icons load

Title: Verify patient dashboard renders correctly on Firefox latest on macOS
Preconditions: Firefox latest installed on macOS; valid clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Firefox latest on macOS | 2. Navigate to OpenMRS | 3. Log in | 4. Open patient dashboard | 5. Compare layout against Chrome baseline
Expected_Result: Dashboard renders identically to baseline within tolerance; no Firefox-specific CSS rendering defects; no console errors

Title: Verify registration form on Microsoft Edge latest on Windows 10
Preconditions: Edge latest installed on Windows 10; registration privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Edge latest | 2. Open patient registration | 3. Fill all required fields | 4. Submit | 5. Verify saved record
Test_Data: Browser=Edge latest; OS=Windows 10; Name=Test PatientA; Gender=F; DOB=1990-01-01
Expected_Result: Registration form submits successfully; no Edge Chromium rendering or autofill anomalies; patient created

Title: Verify clinical forms on Safari latest on macOS
Preconditions: Safari latest on macOS; clinician account; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Safari latest | 2. Open patient with active visit | 3. Start a vitals clinical form | 4. Enter observations | 5. Save
Test_Data: Browser=Safari latest; OS=macOS Sonoma; Pulse=72; BP=120/80
Expected_Result: Form saves; WebKit date/number inputs behave correctly; encounter persists; no Safari-only JS errors

Title: Verify core flows on Chrome prior major version (N-1)
Preconditions: Chrome N-1 version installed; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Chrome N-1 | 2. Log in | 3. Search patient | 4. Open dashboard | 5. Start and save an encounter
Expected_Result: All core flows function on prior version; no regressions vs latest; graceful behavior for any deprecated APIs

Title: Verify core flows on Firefox ESR (prior/extended support)
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Firefox ESR | 2. Log in | 3. Open patient dashboard | 4. Record vitals | 5. Verify save
Expected_Result: All core flows function on ESR; enterprise-locked Firefox remains supported; no errors

Title: Verify Safari prior major version on macOS for clinical workflow
Preconditions: Safari N-1 on macOS; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Safari N-1 | 2. Log in | 3. Open patient | 4. Enter and save diagnosis | 5. Verify rendering
Expected_Result: Workflow completes on prior Safari; no WebKit regression in date pickers or modals

Title: Verify application on Linux (Ubuntu LTS) with Chromium
Preconditions: Chromium on Ubuntu LTS; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Launch Chromium on Ubuntu | 2. Log in | 3. Exercise dashboard, search, encounter entry | 4. Validate fonts render with Linux font stack
Expected_Result: Application functions on Linux; font fallback acceptable; no OS-specific rendering breakage

Title: Pairwise coverage of Browser x OS combinations for login
Preconditions: Multiple browser/OS test rigs available; valid account
Test_Steps: 1. Execute login on each pairwise cell (Chrome/Win, Firefox/macOS, Edge/Win, Safari/macOS, Chromium/Linux) | 2. Record pass/fail per cell | 3. Capture screenshots | 4. Compare
Expected_Result: Login succeeds across all pairwise cells; no combination-specific authentication failure

Title: Pairwise coverage of Browser x OS x Locale for date display
Preconditions: Test rigs with configurable locale; patient with known DOB
Test_Steps: 1. Set locale per cell | 2. Open patient dashboard | 3. Inspect DOB and encounter datetime formatting | 4. Compare expected per locale
Expected_Result: Dates render per locale convention consistently across browser/OS; no MM/DD vs DD/MM ambiguity errors

Title: Verify dashboard at minimum supported desktop resolution 1366x768
Test_Steps: 1. Set viewport to 1366x768 | 2. Open patient dashboard | 3. Verify no horizontal scrollbars on primary content | 4. Check widget overflow
Expected_Result: Layout fits without unintended horizontal scroll; key actions visible above fold; no overlapping elements

Title: Verify dashboard at high resolution 4K (3840x2160) scaling
Preconditions: 4K display with OS scaling at 150 percent; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Set 4K display with 150 percent scaling | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Verify crisp icons and fonts | 4. Check no tiny unscaled text
Expected_Result: UI scales correctly; no blurry raster assets; SVG icons sharp; text legible

Title: Verify responsive behavior at tablet viewport 768px
Preconditions: Tablet emulation in browser devtools; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Set viewport width to 768px | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Verify responsive collapse/menu behavior | 4. Confirm tap targets adequate
Expected_Result: Layout adapts responsively; navigation accessible; no clipped content; tap targets >= 44px

Title: Verify just-below-minimum viewport 1024px boundary handling
Test_Steps: 1. Set viewport to 1023px (just below 1024 breakpoint) | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Set to 1024px exactly | 4. Compare layout switch behavior
Expected_Result: Breakpoint transition is clean at boundary; no flicker or broken intermediate state; correct layout per side

Title: Verify layout integrity at 200 percent browser zoom (WCAG 1.4.4)
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient dashboard | 2. Set browser zoom to 200% | 3. Verify all content and functionality available | 4. Check no content loss or overlap
Expected_Result: All content readable and operable at 200% zoom without horizontal scrolling for primary content; meets WCAG 1.4.4

Title: Verify content reflow at 400 percent zoom (WCAG 1.4.10)
Test_Steps: 1. Open a clinical form | 2. Set zoom to 400% | 3. Verify content reflows to single column | 4. Confirm no two-dimensional scroll for text content
Expected_Result: Content reflows without loss; only one scroll direction for text; meets WCAG 1.4.10 reflow criterion

Title: Verify minimum zoom 50 percent does not break form alignment
Test_Steps: 1. Open registration form | 2. Set zoom to 50% | 3. Inspect field alignment and label association | 4. Submit form
Expected_Result: Form remains usable and submittable at 50% zoom; labels stay associated with inputs

Title: Decision table for locale-driven number, date and name formatting
Preconditions: Locale-switchable account; patients with known data
Test_Steps: 1. For each locale row select locale | 2. Verify date format | 3. Verify decimal/thousand separators in vitals | 4. Verify name display order | 5. Record outcome
Expected_Result: Each locale renders dates, numeric separators and name order per its convention; decision table cells all pass

Title: Verify RTL language rendering (Arabic) mirrors layout
Test_Steps: 1. Switch UI locale to Arabic (ar) | 2. Open dashboard | 3. Verify layout mirrors RTL | 4. Verify numerals and form fields | 5. Check icon directionality
Expected_Result: Layout mirrors right-to-left correctly; text aligned RTL; directional icons flipped; no overlap

Title: Verify UTF-8 multibyte characters in patient names across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Register patient with multibyte name in Chrome | 2. Search same patient in Firefox | 3. Open in Safari | 4. Verify name renders identically
Expected_Result: Multibyte UTF-8 name stored and rendered correctly across all browsers; no mojibake or truncation

Title: Verify encounter datetime displays correctly across client timezones
Preconditions: Two test clients in different timezones; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Record encounter on client in UTC-5 | 2. Note saved timestamp | 3. Open same encounter on client in UTC+9 | 4. Compare displayed local time vs stored UTC
Expected_Result: Encounter datetime stored consistently (server time/UTC) and displayed in each client local timezone correctly

Title: Verify DST boundary datetime handling for appointments
Test_Steps: 1. Set client TZ to a DST-observing zone | 2. Schedule appointment during spring-forward gap (02:30) | 3. Save | 4. Verify stored and displayed time
Expected_Result: Non-existent local time during DST gap is handled gracefully (shifted/rejected with message); no silent corruption

Title: Verify FHIR resource instant fields carry timezone offset across clients
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation via FHIR R4 from client A | 2. Inspect effectiveDateTime offset | 3. GET same from client B different TZ | 4. Compare instant value
Expected_Result: FHIR instant/dateTime fields include correct timezone offset; identical absolute instant regardless of client TZ

Title: Verify session cookie attributes Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite across browsers
Preconditions: HTTPS environment; clinician account; devtools access
Test_Steps: 1. Log in via Chrome | 2. Inspect session cookie attributes in devtools | 3. Repeat in Firefox and Edge | 4. Verify Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite present
Expected_Result: Session cookie has Secure, HttpOnly and appropriate SameSite in all browsers; not readable by JS; not sent over HTTP

Title: Verify session persistence across browser tab and window in same browser
Test_Steps: 1. Log in tab 1 | 2. Open app in tab 2 same browser | 3. Verify shared session | 4. Log out tab 1 | 5. Refresh tab 2
Expected_Result: Session shared across tabs; logout in one tab invalidates session in other on next request; consistent state transition

Title: Verify session isolation between normal and private/incognito window
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as user A in normal window | 2. Open incognito window | 3. Log in as user B | 4. Verify sessions isolated | 5. Confirm no cookie leakage
Expected_Result: Sessions fully isolated; user B context never reflects user A; no PHI cross-contamination between contexts

Title: Verify session timeout and re-auth behavior across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Leave idle past timeout | 3. Attempt a clinical action | 4. Verify redirect to login | 5. Re-authenticate and resume
Expected_Result: Idle session expires; protected action triggers re-auth; unsaved data warning shown where applicable; no data exposure post-timeout

Title: Verify behavior when third-party cookies are blocked
Preconditions: Browser configured to block third-party cookies
Test_Steps: 1. Block third-party cookies in browser settings | 2. Log in | 3. Use core flows | 4. Verify no broken embedded content or SSO
Expected_Result: First-party session functions; any third-party embeds degrade gracefully with clear messaging; no silent failure

Title: Verify cookies disabled entirely yields graceful error
Test_Steps: 1. Disable all cookies | 2. Attempt login | 3. Observe behavior | 4. Verify clear user-facing message
Expected_Result: Login fails gracefully with message indicating cookies are required; no infinite redirect loop or stack trace

Title: Verify patient summary PDF download in Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari
Preconditions: Clinician account; patient with data; download privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient summary | 2. Trigger PDF export in each browser | 3. Verify file downloads | 4. Open and validate content integrity
Expected_Result: PDF downloads in all browsers with correct filename and content-type; file opens; PHI rendered correctly; no corruption

Title: Verify CSV report export encoding and line endings across OS
Test_Steps: 1. Export a report as CSV on Windows | 2. Export same on macOS | 3. Open both in spreadsheet app | 4. Compare encoding and delimiters
Expected_Result: CSV exports with UTF-8 encoding and consistent delimiters; opens without mojibake; line endings handled cross-OS

Title: Verify large file download (>50MB export) completes without timeout
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger large export (>50MB) in Chrome | 2. Monitor download progress | 3. Repeat in Firefox | 4. Verify file complete and valid
Expected_Result: Large download completes without browser/server timeout; file size matches; no truncation

Title: Verify download filename and Content-Disposition handling across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger export | 2. Inspect Content-Disposition header | 3. Verify suggested filename honored in each browser | 4. Check special chars in filename
Expected_Result: Filename from Content-Disposition honored consistently; special characters sanitized; no default index.html naming

Title: Verify attachment upload (clinical document) across browsers
Preconditions: Clinician account; patient context; attachment privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient attachments | 2. Upload a JPG in Chrome | 3. Repeat in Firefox, Edge, Safari | 4. Verify thumbnail and stored file
Expected_Result: Upload succeeds in all browsers; file stored and viewable; correct MIME detected; no browser-specific picker failure

Title: Verify oversized file upload rejected with clear message (boundary)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt upload of file 1 byte over max limit | 2. Observe rejection | 3. Attempt file exactly at limit | 4. Verify accepted
Expected_Result: Over-limit file rejected with clear size message; at-limit file accepted; boundary handled precisely; no partial upload stored

Title: Verify disallowed file type (executable) is rejected across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to upload .exe disguised as .jpg | 2. Attempt genuine .exe | 3. Verify both rejected | 4. Confirm server-side validation not just client
Expected_Result: Disallowed types rejected by server-side content validation; no execution; clear error; no bypass via extension rename

Title: Verify drag-and-drop upload works where supported and degrades otherwise
Test_Steps: 1. Drag-and-drop file onto upload zone in Chrome | 2. Repeat in Safari | 3. Verify fallback to file picker where DnD unsupported
Expected_Result: Drag-and-drop works where supported; graceful fallback to standard picker; no broken UI in any browser

Title: Verify CSS Grid/Flexbox layout parity Blink vs Gecko vs WebKit
Test_Steps: 1. Capture dashboard screenshot in Chrome (Blink) | 2. Capture in Firefox (Gecko) | 3. Capture in Safari (WebKit) | 4. Pixel-diff key components
Expected_Result: Layout parity within tolerance across engines; no engine-specific grid/flex collapse; differences only in expected font rendering

Title: Verify date/time input control behavior differs gracefully across engines
Preconditions: Clinician account; form with date input
Test_Steps: 1. Open native HTML date input in Chrome | 2. Open in Firefox | 3. Open in Safari (WebKit historically limited) | 4. Verify valid entry in each
Expected_Result: Date entry succeeds in all engines via native or polyfill control; invalid input rejected; no engine left without usable picker

Title: Verify no unhandled console errors across browser matrix on critical pages
Test_Steps: 1. Open login, dashboard, registration, form pages | 2. Monitor console in Chrome | 3. Repeat across Firefox, Edge, Safari | 4. Log any errors
Expected_Result: No uncaught JS exceptions or 404 asset errors in console on any browser; warnings triaged; critical paths clean

Title: Verify graceful degradation when JavaScript is disabled
Test_Steps: 1. Disable JavaScript | 2. Load application | 3. Observe behavior | 4. Verify informative noscript message
Expected_Result: Application shows a clear noscript notice requiring JavaScript; no blank page or silent failure; no PHI leak in static HTML

Title: Verify static asset caching headers consistent across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Load app fresh in Chrome | 2. Inspect cache-control/etag on assets | 3. Reload and verify 304s | 4. Repeat in Firefox
Expected_Result: Cache headers honored consistently; cacheable assets return 304 on revalidation; no stale PHI cached inappropriately

Title: Verify back/forward cache (bfcache) does not expose stale PHI after logout
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient PHI page | 2. Log out | 3. Press browser Back | 4. Verify PHI not shown from bfcache | 5. Repeat across browsers
Expected_Result: Back after logout does not render cached PHI; page forces re-auth or shows blocked state; no PHI exposure via bfcache

Title: Verify FHIR API content negotiation across HTTP clients/browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Request Patient with Accept application/fhir+json | 2. Request with application/json | 3. Request XML if supported | 4. Verify correct content-type response
Expected_Result: Server honors content negotiation; returns valid FHIR R4 resource with matching content-type; consistent across client tools

Title: Verify SMART app launch in embedded iframe across browsers (cookie/storage)
Preconditions: SMART on FHIR app registered; clinician account
Test_Steps: 1. Launch SMART app in EHR context in Chrome | 2. Verify OAuth handshake and token | 3. Repeat in Safari (ITP storage partitioning) | 4. Confirm app loads
Expected_Result: SMART app launches and authorizes in all browsers; storage partitioning/ITP does not break token exchange; scopes enforced

Title: Verify HL7 v2 message ingestion unaffected by client browser/OS
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine endpoint; integration account
Test_Steps: 1. Send HL7 v2 ADT message from integration client on Windows | 2. Send equivalent from Linux client | 3. Verify patient updated identically | 4. Check encoding (MSH-18)
Expected_Result: HL7 ingestion is client-OS independent; character encoding per MSH-18 preserved; resulting record identical regardless of source OS

Title: Verify data written via one browser is consistent when read via another
Preconditions: Clinician account; two browsers; DB read access
Test_Steps: 1. Create encounter with obs in Chrome | 2. Query backing record via API/DB | 3. Read same encounter in Firefox UI | 4. Compare values
Expected_Result: Persisted data identical regardless of authoring browser; no browser-introduced encoding or rounding differences; referential integrity intact

Title: Verify print stylesheet renders patient summary correctly across browsers
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient summary | 2. Invoke print preview in Chrome | 3. Repeat in Firefox, Edge, Safari | 4. Verify print layout, page breaks, no nav chrome
Expected_Result: Print preview applies print stylesheet; PHI legible; nav/buttons hidden; clean page breaks; consistent across browsers

Title: Verify Resource Adapter Layer maps fields identically when SUT swapped to OpenEMR/HAPI
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured; OpenEMR/HAPI FHIR targets available
Test_Steps: 1. Run patient read via adapter against OpenMRS | 2. Against HAPI FHIR | 3. Against OpenEMR | 4. Compare normalized field mapping output
Expected_Result: Adapter normalizes equivalent fields across target systems; no field drops or mismaps; portability contract upheld

Title: Verify screen reader announces dashboard landmarks across browser/AT pairs
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate dashboard with NVDA+Firefox | 2. With JAWS+Chrome | 3. With VoiceOver+Safari | 4. Verify landmark and heading announcements
Expected_Result: Landmarks, headings and controls announced consistently across AT/browser pairs; meets WCAG 4.1.2 name/role/value
